summary: Three targeted fixes addressing greptile-apps[bot] review #3 feedback: (1) Keystone Dockerfile now co-locates extras with the local source path in a single uv pip install argument, eliminating potential PyPI resolution ambiguity; (2) apply-constraint-overrides.sh gains a symmetric pre-flight existence check for upper-constraints.txt; (3) Test 5 adds assert_file_not_contains for replaced versions, achieving assertion parity with Test 2. All 15 shell tests pass. No new bugs introduced.

tests_checklist.item: Tests exist and pass (15/15 assertions pass across 5 test functions)
Edge cases covered (no-op, replacement, removal, comments/blanks, multiple overrides)
Negative assertions verify old state removed after mutation (Test 2 and Test 5)

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing code patterns (symmetric validation, assertion consistency)
Changes are minimal and scoped to reviewer feedback
No dead code or unnecessary additions

security_checklist.item: Co-located extras eliminate PyPI resolution ambiguity for local path installs
Pre-flight validation prevents cryptic downstream errors

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible — each fix is 1-4 lines

fail_fast_checklist.item: Constraints file missing detected early with actionable error message

defensive_checklist.item: All file inputs validated before use (constraints and overrides)
Error output goes to stderr with exit 1

suggested_improvements: D4 (minor): Consider adding a dedicated test case for the new constraints file missing scenario (e.g., test_missing_constraints_file_fails) to directly exercise the new pre-flight check at scripts/apply-constraint-overrides.sh:19-22. Currently this path is not directly tested, though the check is straightforward.

DA3 (minor): The extra-packages.yaml lists extras as separate entries (keystone[ldap], keystone[memcache_pool], keystone[oauth1]) while the Dockerfile now combines them into a single argument (/tmp/keystone[ldap,memcache_pool,oauth1]). Consider adding a comment in extra-packages.yaml or the Dockerfile noting this is intentional — the YAML is a reference manifest, not a direct input to pip install.

metadata.title: C001: Add Keystone container image and release config
